faq.label: Common Questions
faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Learn to GPT is the gamified learning platform for ChatGPT. It teaches you to master ChatGPT through interactive lessons, hands-on exercises, and real-world projects — from your first prompt to building Custom GPTs. With 5 learning tracks, 175+ lessons, and 700+ exercises, it's the most comprehensive ChatGPT training available.
faq.a2: Yes. Track 1 (Foundations) is completely free — no credit card required. You'll learn how ChatGPT thinks, write your first prompts, and master conversation patterns. All 5 tracks, 175+ lessons, 700+ exercises, achievements, and the leaderboard are free.
faq.a3: Five learning tracks take you from beginner to expert: (1) Foundations — how ChatGPT works and first prompts, (2) Professional Prompting — chain-of-thought, structured output, few-shot, (3) Custom GPTs — system prompts, knowledge files, and Actions, (4) API & Agents — tool use and agentic workflows, (5) Architect — evals, monitoring, and enterprise patterns.
faq.a4: No. Learn to GPT is an independent learning platform. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or officially connected to OpenAI. We teach ChatGPT skills based on publicly available documentation and real-world building experience.
faq.q5: What languages is Learn to GPT available in?
faq.a5: Learn to GPT is available in 7 languages: English, Japanese, Korean, Simplified Chinese, German, French, and Spanish. The full UI, all lessons, and exercises are translated so you can learn ChatGPT in your preferred language.
faq.q6: What is a Custom GPT and how do I build one?
faq.a6: A Custom GPT is a personalized version of ChatGPT you configure with a system prompt, uploaded knowledge files, and API Actions. Learn to GPT's Track 3 teaches Custom GPTs hands-on: GPT Builder, system prompt design, knowledge file upload, Action integration, and publishing to the GPT Store. Track 6 covers daily setup and workflow integration.
faq.q7: How long does it take to complete Learn to GPT?
faq.a7: Track 1 (Foundations) takes about 2-3 hours and gets you writing effective ChatGPT prompts immediately. The full curriculum across all tracks can be completed in 20-40 hours depending on your pace. Each lesson is self-contained so you can learn in short sessions.
faq.q8: Do I need programming experience to use Learn to GPT?
faq.a8: No programming experience is required for Tracks 1 and 2, which cover ChatGPT fundamentals and professional prompting. Tracks 3-5 involve ChatGPT and the API, which benefit from basic coding familiarity, but the lessons teach you step by step.
faq.q9: What is the best way to learn ChatGPT in 2025?
faq.a9: The best way to learn ChatGPT is through structured, hands-on practice with real-world scenarios. Learn to GPT provides exactly this: interactive exercises where you write real prompts, get instant feedback, and build actual projects. Reading documentation alone doesn't build skill — repetition with feedback does.
faq.q10: Is Learn to GPT better than YouTube tutorials for learning ChatGPT?
faq.a10: Learn to GPT is designed for active learning, not passive watching. YouTube tutorials show you what someone else did; Learn to GPT makes you do it yourself in a live sandbox. Research shows hands-on learners retain 3x more than video watchers. Every lesson scores your prompts and explains what worked and what didn't.

claude-code.heading: Build Your Own Custom GPT
claude-code.subheading: Design, configure, and publish a ChatGPT that does exactly what you need
claude-code.intro: Custom GPTs let you create a personalized version of ChatGPT for any use case. Learn how to write effective system prompts, attach knowledge files, connect APIs with Actions, and publish to the GPT Store — with hands-on exercises at every step.
claude-code.cta: Stop reading about it. Build something.

whatIs.heading: What is a Custom GPT?
whatIs.body: A Custom GPT is a personalized version of ChatGPT you configure for a specific purpose. You give it a name, a system prompt that shapes its behavior, knowledge files it can reference, and Actions that connect it to external APIs. When you publish it, anyone can use it — just like a standard ChatGPT conversation, but specialized for your exact use case.

0.title: System Prompt Design
0.desc: Write system instructions that shape your GPT's behavior, tone, and capabilities reliably across every conversation.

1.title: Knowledge Files
1.desc: Upload PDFs, documents, and data files that your Custom GPT can search and reference when answering questions.

2.title: Actions and API Integration
2.desc: Connect your GPT to external APIs so it can fetch live data, submit forms, or trigger workflows on behalf of users.

3.title: Testing and Evaluation
3.desc: Test your Custom GPT for edge cases and failure modes before publishing. Iterate on the system prompt until behavior is reliable.

4.title: GPT Store Publishing
4.desc: Publish your Custom GPT to the GPT Store, manage visibility, and distribute to your team or the public.

5.title: Advanced Patterns
5.desc: Chain multiple Actions, build retrieval-augmented GPTs, and design conversation flows that handle complex multi-step tasks.

preview.caption: Learn to build a Custom GPT that handles real workflows

directAnswer.text: A Custom GPT is a version of ChatGPT you configure for a specific purpose using OpenAI's GPT Builder. You give it a system prompt, optional knowledge files, and Actions that connect it to external APIs. Once published, it behaves like a specialized AI assistant anyone can interact with through a standard ChatGPT conversation interface.

howToUse.label: Getting Started
howToUse.heading: How to Build a Custom GPT

step1.title: Open the GPT Builder
step1.body: Go to chatgpt.com and click 'Explore GPTs' then 'Create'. You need a ChatGPT Plus, Team, or Enterprise subscription to build and publish Custom GPTs.

step2.title: Write your system prompt
step2.body: In the Configure tab, write a system prompt that describes what your GPT does, how it behaves, and any constraints. Be specific — the system prompt is the biggest lever for GPT quality.

step3.title: Add knowledge and Actions
step3.body: Upload knowledge files your GPT should reference (PDFs, docs, data). Add Actions by providing an OpenAPI schema for any external API you want the GPT to call.

step4.title: Test, refine, and publish
step4.body: Use the Preview panel to test your GPT against real user questions. When behavior is consistent and accurate, publish it — privately, to your team, or to the GPT Store.

workflow1.title: Customer support GPT
workflow1.body: Upload your product docs, FAQs, and troubleshooting guides. The GPT answers support questions accurately from your knowledge base without hallucinating.

workflow2.title: Internal knowledge base
workflow2.body: Upload company policies, SOPs, and process docs. Your team GPT answers internal questions 24/7 without someone having to dig through Confluence or Notion.

workflow3.title: Data lookup and live APIs
workflow3.body: Connect a Custom GPT to your API with Actions. Users ask in plain English, the GPT fetches live data, and returns a formatted answer — no dashboard required.

workflow4.title: Specialized writing assistant
workflow4.body: Configure a GPT with your brand voice, editorial guidelines, and style rules. It drafts on-brand content consistently without needing a system prompt in every chat.

claudeMdExplained.heading: What makes a great Custom GPT system prompt?
claudeMdExplained.body: The system prompt is the most important part of any Custom GPT. It defines the GPT's purpose, persona, constraints, and output format. A well-written system prompt produces consistent, reliable responses. A vague one produces inconsistent behavior that frustrates users.

tip1.title: Define the role clearly
tip1.body: Start with who the GPT is and what it does. 'You are a customer support assistant for Acme Corp. You answer questions about our SaaS product using the knowledge files provided. If you don't know something, say so.'

tip2.title: Set explicit constraints
tip2.body: Tell the GPT what NOT to do. 'Never make up pricing. Never discuss competitors. If asked about topics outside your scope, redirect to our support team at support@company.com.'

tip3.title: Specify output format
tip3.body: If you want structured responses, specify the format. 'Always respond with a brief answer first, then bullet-point supporting details. Keep responses under 200 words unless the user asks for more detail.'

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Do I need ChatGPT Plus to build Custom GPTs?
faq.a1: Yes. Building and publishing Custom GPTs requires a ChatGPT Plus, Team, or Enterprise subscription. Free users can use Custom GPTs that others have published, but cannot create their own.
faq.q2: What can I put in a Custom GPT knowledge file?
faq.a2: Custom GPTs accept PDFs, Word documents, text files, and spreadsheets up to 512MB total. The GPT uses retrieval-augmented generation to search and reference the content when answering questions.
faq.q3: What are Actions in a Custom GPT?
faq.a3: Actions let your Custom GPT call external APIs. You provide an OpenAPI schema describing the API, and the GPT automatically decides when to call it based on the user's request. Common uses: fetch live data, submit forms, trigger workflows.
faq.q4: How is a Custom GPT different from just using a system prompt?
faq.a4: A Custom GPT packages a system prompt, knowledge files, and Actions into a reusable, shareable product. Anyone can open the GPT and start a fresh conversation with all your configuration pre-applied — no copy-pasting a system prompt every time.

meta.title: Best Prompt Engineering Course for ChatGPT | Learn to GPT
meta.description: Master prompt engineering for ChatGPT. Learn context management, system prompts, few-shot examples, and advanced techniques with hands-on exercises.

prompt-engineering.heading: Get Better Results from Every ChatGPT Prompt
prompt-engineering.subheading: Master the art of communicating with AI
prompt-engineering.intro: Learn how to write effective prompts that get the best results from ChatGPT. From basic conversation techniques to advanced strategies like chain-of-thought reasoning and tool use.

whatIs.heading: What is Prompt Engineering?
whatIs.body: Prompt engineering is the skill of crafting instructions that help AI models produce accurate, useful, and relevant outputs. With ChatGPT, good prompts mean better code, clearer analysis, and more reliable results.

0.title: Context Window Management
0.desc: Learn to structure conversations and manage ChatGPT's context window for optimal results.

1.title: System Prompts
1.desc: Design effective system prompts that set the right tone, constraints, and behavior.

2.title: Few-Shot Examples
2.desc: Use examples to guide ChatGPT's output format, style, and reasoning patterns.

3.title: Chain-of-Thought
3.desc: Enable step-by-step reasoning for complex problems and multi-step tasks.

4.title: Tool Use
4.desc: Integrate ChatGPT with external tools and APIs for real-world automation.

5.title: Advanced Patterns
5.desc: Master techniques like constitutional AI, self-reflection, and multi-turn strategies.

prompt-engineering.trackInfo: This content is covered across our beginner and intermediate tracks.

directAnswer.text: Prompt engineering is the practice of writing clear, structured instructions that guide AI models like ChatGPT to produce accurate, useful outputs. It is the single highest-leverage skill for anyone working with AI — the difference between generic responses and production-quality results comes down to how well you communicate what you need.

techniques.label: Core Techniques
techniques.heading: 5 Prompt Engineering Techniques That Actually Work

technique1.title: Give ChatGPT a specific role
technique1.body: Instead of asking a general question, define who ChatGPT is in the context of your task. "You are a senior data analyst at a healthcare startup" produces dramatically different output than a bare question. The role sets vocabulary, depth, and perspective.
technique1.example: You are a senior TypeScript engineer. Review this code for security vulnerabilities, missing error handling, and performance issues. Be specific about line numbers.

technique2.title: Structure your output format
technique2.body: Tell ChatGPT exactly what the response should look like. Specify JSON keys, bullet point structure, section headings, or word count limits. ChatGPT follows format constraints reliably when they are explicit in the prompt.
technique2.example: Respond in JSON with keys: summary (string, under 50 words), risk_level ("low" | "medium" | "high"), and action_items (array of strings).

technique3.title: Use chain-of-thought reasoning
technique3.body: For complex problems, ask ChatGPT to think step by step before giving a final answer. This produces more accurate results on math, logic, and multi-step analysis because it forces the model to show intermediate reasoning.
technique3.example: Think through this step by step. First, identify the core problem. Then list possible solutions with trade-offs. Finally, recommend the best option and explain why.

technique4.title: Provide few-shot examples
technique4.body: Show ChatGPT one or two input-output examples before asking for the real task. This calibrates tone, format, and reasoning style faster than any instruction paragraph. One good example is worth 300 words of explanation.
technique4.example: Here is an example of the format I want: Input: 'The server crashed at 3am' -> Output: {severity: 'critical', category: 'infrastructure', action: 'page on-call'}. Now classify this: 'User cannot reset password'

technique5.title: Set explicit constraints
technique5.body: Tell ChatGPT what NOT to do. "Do not use jargon." "Do not exceed 200 words." "If you are uncertain, say so instead of guessing." Negative constraints prevent the most common failure modes — over-confidence, verbosity, and off-topic drift.
technique5.example: Constraints: Never use em dashes. No summary paragraph at the end. If you lack information to answer confidently, say exactly what additional context you would need.

mistake1.title: Being too vague
mistake1.good: Write a 3-paragraph follow-up email to a job application for a marketing manager role. Professional but warm tone. End with a specific meeting request, not an open-ended question.
mistake1.why: Vague prompts produce generic output. Specificity on audience, tone, length, and purpose improves quality immediately.

mistake2.title: Not providing context
mistake2.good: Here is Q3 revenue data for a B2B SaaS startup with 200 customers. Identify the most important trend, what data is missing that would change the interpretation, and what decisions this data supports.
mistake2.why: ChatGPT cannot infer your industry, audience, or goals. Context turns analysis from surface-level to actionable.

mistake3.title: Asking for everything at once
mistake3.bad: Write a complete marketing strategy with content calendar, ad copy, email sequences, and brand guidelines.
mistake3.good: Let's build a content strategy. Start with: who is the target audience, what are the 3 main content pillars, and what is one piece of content per pillar we should create first?
mistake3.why: Breaking complex tasks into steps lets you review and course-correct. One massive prompt produces shallow output across the board.

faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.q1: Do I need to learn prompt engineering?
faq.a1: Yes — if you use AI at all. Prompt engineering is not a niche skill. It is the difference between AI that saves you time and AI that wastes it. The ROI on learning to write clear, structured prompts is immediate and compounds with every interaction.
faq.q2: What is the difference between a prompt and a system prompt?
faq.a2: A prompt is what you type in a conversation. A system prompt is a set of instructions that runs before any conversation — it defines ChatGPT's role, output format, constraints, and standing context. System prompts shape every response ChatGPT gives.
faq.q3: Does prompt engineering work the same for all AI models?
faq.a3: The core principles — specificity, structure, examples, constraints — transfer across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and other models. The details differ: ChatGPT responds especially well to role-setting, explicit format contracts, and structured step-by-step instructions.
faq.q4: How long should a prompt be?
faq.a4: As long as it needs to be. A well-structured 500-word prompt with context, examples, and constraints will outperform a 20-word prompt every time. Length is not the issue — clarity and structure are.

finalCta.headline: Ready to master prompt engineering?
finalCta.subtitle: Start with free interactive lessons today.
